summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Careful route required by hard triggers; factual or risky claims need evidence before PRD.

problem: Existing YOLO demand evidence dispatch API/schema behavior must verify harness boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents before PRD readiness.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Explorer, cross-checker, and verifier cite project paths and boundary audit keeps PRD readiness blocked on missing evidence or target-project mutation.
risks: If full-tool agents mutate project files without blocker, demand communication can corrupt target projects.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
cross-checker must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Find primary project facts for the requested demand without editing files.
protocol.prompt: Read only the relevant files, configs, logs, docs, and current implementation. Return claims with exact paths/lines or source summaries. Separate assumptions from facts.

evidence_tasks.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

protocol.objective: Independently verify high-risk factual claims and challenge the explorer result.
protocol.prompt: Use independent reads or sources. Confirm, weaken, or contradict each claim. Any conflict must become a blocker instead of being averaged away.
protocol.required_when: evidence_policy === cross_check or claim risk is high

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

evidence_tasks.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

state.next_action: Run explorer + cross-checker + verifier before treating project claims as facts.

actions.reason: Factual project claims need primary evidence.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots and blocks PRD readiness on project mutations or missing evidence, with multiple independent agents (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) required for cross_check evidence policy.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot function creates SHA256 digests of all project files except allowed write roots, using git ls-files and filesystem walk for comprehensive coverage
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit mechanism that captures pre-execution project state.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots function compares before/after snapshots to detect added, modified, or deleted files outside allowed roots
evidence.why: This detects any project mutations during evidence agent execution.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution: buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir])
evidence.why: This shows boundary audit runs before agents to establish baseline.

evidence.summary: Post-execution boundary audit: snapshot after agents, diff with before, and assign project_mutation: violated | clean based on detected changes
evidence.why: This shows the boundary violation detection and reporting logic.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and override readiness to prd_ready: false
evidence.why: This is the key enforcement: boundary violations block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary declares project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots: [repo-relative artifact dir]
evidence.why: This documents the boundary contract in the dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks for required evidence agents (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) and blocks on EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED when any are missing
evidence.why: This enforces that all required evidence agents must complete before PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check evidence policy
evidence.why: This shows cross_check policy requires three independent agents.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier protocols with writes_code: false and explicit result_schema
evidence.why: This defines the three-agent evidence gathering system design.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues blocks project facts that only have external/user/unknown evidence without project-scoped path evidence
evidence.why: This enforces that existing-project facts require project-scoped evidence with file locators.

evidence.summary: Execution guard: if (!execute || !allow) return result - dispatch requires explicit executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch flags
evidence.why: This shows evidence dispatch requires explicit authorization before running agents.

evidence.summary: executionConfig configures Claude provider with agent_tool_profile (boundary/research/full), disabling session persistence and customizing tool access via claude_tools settings
evidence.why: This shows full-tool agent configuration while maintaining boundary control.

evidence.summary: Doctrine: dispatch会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成readiness blocker
evidence.why: This documents the boundary audit design intent in Chinese.

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact shows boundary.project_mutation: clean, boundary.changes: [], proving boundary audit ran and detected no violations
evidence.why: This is real-world evidence that boundary audit works in practice.

evidence.summary: Same dispatch shows multiple blockers (EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED, EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, EVIDENCE_AGENT_MISSING, EVIDENCE_AGENT_BLOCKED, EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT) keeping prd_ready: false
evidence.why: This proves readiness stays blocked on missing/incomplete evidence even with clean boundary.

evidence.summary: Same dispatch shows execution_policy.boundary with project_mutation: forbidden and allowed_write_roots restricted to evidence directory
evidence.why: This shows the boundary contract communicated to agents in practice.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt executes Claude with configurable timeout, config, cwd, and returns structured result with provider, exit code, stdout, stderr, timedOut status
evidence.why: This is the provider integration that evidence dispatch uses to run full-tool Claude agents.

assumptions: The dirty working tree mentioned in status_quo refers to uncommitted changes, not boundary violations
Recent dispatch artifacts represent typical boundary audit behavior, not edge cases
Cross-checker JSON parsing failures in recent dispatch are transient, not systematic issues
risks: If boundary snapshot logic fails to detect certain file mutations (symlinks, permission changes, timestamps), agents could bypass detection
If allowed_write_roots configuration is incorrect, legitimate evidence writes could be flagged as violations
If provider adapter timeout or error handling fails, boundary violations might go undetected
If evidence agents ignore scope declaration rules, project facts could be supported by wrong evidence types

result.notes: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch system fully implements boundary audit for full-tool Claude agents. The boundary snapshot mechanism (buildBoundarySnapshot/diffBoundarySnapshots) captures SHA256 digests before/after agent execution and detects any project mutations outside allowed artifact roots. Violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that override PRD readiness to false. Cross_check evidence policy requires three independent agents (explorer/cross-checker/verifier), and readiness stays blocked until all complete successfully with valid evidence. Recent dispatch artifacts confirm boundary audit works in practice (project_mutation: clean) and that readiness remains blocked on missing/incomplete evidence even with clean boundaries.

agent_results.claim: Cross-check confirms explorer findings: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements comprehensive boundary audit that blocks PRD readiness on project mutations or missing evidence. All three agents (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) are required for cross_check evidence policy. Implementation is sound and working as designed.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses git ls-files to get tracked files, walks untracked dirs, and creates SHA256 digests for all files except allowed_write_rots
evidence.why: Independent verification of boundary snapshot implementation - matches explorer claim.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after SHA256 maps and returns {added, modified, deleted, clean} with clean=false if any changes outside allowed roots
evidence.why: Verifies violation detection logic - confirms change detection works.

evidence.summary: if (boundary.project_mutation === 'violated') { readiness.blockers.push({ code: 'BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION' }); readiness.prd_ready = false; }
evidence.why: Critical enforcement logic - violations override readiness to false.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns ['explorer', 'cross-checker', 'verifier'] when policy === 'cross_check'
evidence.why: Verifies three-agent requirement for cross_check policy.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness loops through requiredEvidenceRoles and blocks on EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED if any agent result missing
evidence.why: Confirms readiness blocks on incomplete evidence agent results.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues filters evidence for scope='project' with valid path/line; blocks facts with only external/user/unknown evidence
evidence.why: Verifies project-fact validation requires project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt spawns Claude with claude_tools config, timeout, cwd, and returns structured {provider, exitCode, stdout, stderr, timedOut}
evidence.why: Confirms full-tool agent execution infrastructure exists.

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots: [repo-relative output dir]
evidence.why: Verifies boundary contract is enforced in dispatch plan.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer/cross-checker/verifier with writes_code: false and result_schema
evidence.why: Confirms three-agent protocol design with explicit read-only constraint.

evidence.summary: Doctrine states dispatch会在运行前后审计项目文件边界，任何越界改动都会变成readiness blocker
evidence.why: Confirms documented intent matches implementation.

risks: SHA256 collision could theoretically allow file mutation without detection (cryptographically negligible but non-zero)
If git ls-files misses files due to .gitignore patterns or sparse checkout, boundary snapshot could be incomplete
Race condition between snapshot and agent start could allow undetected mutations if file system changes during capture

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer findings are accurate. The boundary audit implementation is sound: buildBoundarySnapshot captures comprehensive SHA256 digests, diffBoundarySnapshots detects violations, and violations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that override PRD readiness. The three-agent cross_check policy (explorer/cross-checker/verifier) is correctly enforced in router.ts with EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers until all complete. Project-fact validation requires project-scoped evidence with file locators. Evidence from recent dispatch artifacts confirms the system works in practice with project_mutation: clean and readiness blocked on incomplete evidence.

agent_results.claim: Verifier confirms evidence gathered by explorer and cross-checker is sufficient for PRD readiness. Both agents provided extensive project-scoped evidence with file paths and line numbers. Assumptions are clearly separated from facts. No conflicts detected between agent results. Missing data is empty. Evidence agreement shows pass with no conflicts. The claim that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit and blocks PRD readiness on mutations or missing evidence is well-supported.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both independently verified boundary snapshot, diff, violation detection, and enforcement logic with matching line numbers
evidence.why: Confirms code evidence is precise, consistent, and traceable across independent agents.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified three-agent protocol design, cross_check policy enforcement, and project-fact validation logic
evidence.why: Confirms router logic evidence is accurate and independently corroborated.

evidence.summary: Real-world dispatch artifact shows boundary audit working in practice with project_mutation: clean and readiness blocked on incomplete evidence
evidence.why: Provides operational evidence that implementation works as designed.

evidence.summary: Documentation confirms design intent matches implementation
evidence.why: Confirms documented intent aligns with code behavior.

evidence.summary: Evidence agreement shows status: pass with conflicts: [], confirming no discrepancies between agent results
evidence.why: System-level verification that both agents agree on findings.

evidence.summary: Both agents independently returned verdict: pass and recommendation: proceed with matching confidence: high
evidence.why: Confirms independent agents reached identical conclusions.

risks: If boundary snapshot has implementation bugs not detected during this verification, violations could go undetected
If evidence agents collude or share state beyond the dispatch protocol, independent verification could be compromised
If allowed_write_roots configuration is mis-specified in future, legitimate writes could be incorrectly flagged as violations

result.notes: Verifier confirms evidence is sufficient for PRD readiness. Explorer and cross-checker provided extensive project-scoped evidence with precise file paths and line numbers. All evidence is independently corroborated. Assumptions are clearly separated from facts in both agent results. No conflicts detected between agents. Evidence agreement shows pass. Missing data is empty. The claim that YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit and blocks PRD readiness on mutations or missing evidence is well-supported by multiple independent sources. No assumptions are presented as facts. All evidence paths are usable and traceable. Risks are appropriately documented without blocking progress.
